text.title: cloudflare-domain-dns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cloudflare-domain-dns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding only an A record on a dual-stack origin and forgetting AAAA. The server has working IPv6 (Hetzner, OVH, DigitalOcean default), but the Cloudflare zone publishes IPv4 only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cloudflare's edge will still proxy over IPv6 to the origin if AAAA is published; if it is not published, IPv6 clients still reach Cloudflare (anycast covers v6) but you lose the option to publish IPv6-only services and you create asymmetry between A and AAAA reverse-DNS expectations. Worse, if you later disable proxy (gray cloud) for a record without adding AAAA, IPv6 clients stop resolving entirely while IPv4 users see no issue — diagnosis takes hours.

failure-mode.description: Pointing the apex A record at Cloudflare while leaving an old A record in another DNS provider's zone (split horizon caused by stale registrar NS).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Until the registry NS swap propagates, half the world resolves via the old provider (stale IP) and half via Cloudflare (correct IP). Users see intermittent failures for up to 48h. Always verify `dig NS example.com +short` from multiple resolvers before declaring migration done.
